name: amys lentil soup
instructions: Sauté the soffrito (yellow onion, celery, carrots) in olive oil. Add pureed portabella mushrooms and cook them down. Add exactly ONE finely chopped tomato — this dish should NOT taste like tomatoes; the tomato is structural body, not a flavor headliner. Cook the tomato down. Deglaze with white wine and let it reduce. Add lentils, rosemary, black pepper, and enough water to cover. Slow cook covered for a long time. If the broth is too thin at the end, stir in a slurry of starch to thicken.
notes:
ONE TOMATO, NOT MANY (TOMATO AS A BUDDY): this dish should not taste like tomatoes. A single finely chopped tomato is enough — it's there for tomato BALANCE with the mushroom rather than for tomato flavor. The mushroom is the headliner and it wants a buddy, but not a loud one. Pureeing the tomato or adding more pulls the dish toward a tomato-lentil soup, which is a different recipe entirely.

NO TOMATO PASTE: tomato paste in particular pushes the dish too tomato-flavored. It's concentrated, sweet, and amplifies tomato presence beyond what this recipe wants. Stick to the single fresh chopped tomato.

NO THYME: thyme is inappropriate here. The dish is mushroom-and-lentil dominant — a relatively monolithic savory profile — so it wants rosemary (which adds a second dimension) rather than thyme (which unifies scattered flavors). See the herbs and greens article on the thyme-vs-rosemary axis.

NO OLD BAY: removed from the recipe. Old bay belongs in italian-grandma-soup-shaped dishes that explicitly choose it as one of the three flavoring directions. This dish has its own flavor backbone (mushroom + lentil + wine + rosemary) and doesn't want the old bay axis on top.

HERB DOSING:
- Rosemary helps a little. Use lightly — it's the second-dimension herb that makes the dish less flat, but rosemary at full quantity would dominate.
- Thyme is inappropriate.
- Don't reach for other herbs by default.

WHITE WINE IS LOAD-BEARING: a real white wine deglaze helps a lot. The acid and depth it adds are what lifts the dish above a generic lentil stew. Don't substitute vinegar.

STARCH FINISH (OPTIONAL): if the broth ends up thinner than you want, stir in a small starch slurry at the end to thicken. Not always needed — the pureed mushrooms and lentils usually carry enough body — but it's the right move when the soup is too brothy.
